commit.message:
Pre-migration: save uncommitted changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(runner): add reactive filter and flatMap array builtins (#2977)

* feat(runner): add reactive filter and flatMap array builtins

Filter runs a predicate pattern per element and outputs a shorter array
of original cell references where the predicate is truthy. FlatMap runs
a pattern per element that returns an array and concatenates all results
one level deep.

Both use identity-based reconciliation (same as map), skip sparse array
holes, and support WithPattern variants for the compiler transform.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): remove filter/flatMap from IDerivable to avoid type conflict

.filter() and .flatMap() on IDerivable<T> conflict with
Array.prototype.filter/flatMap on OpaqueRef<T[]> (which is an
intersection of OpaqueCell<T[]> & Array<…>). TypeScript picks the
IDerivable overload first, cascading wrong return types through
chained calls. Keep only filterWithPattern/flatMapWithPattern on
the type interface; the runtime methods still exist on Cell.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: review feedback for filter/flatMap builtins

- Symmetric element cell IDs: map now uses { map: result, elementKey }
  matching filter/flatMap convention
- Fix flatMapWithPattern generic: pattern returns S[] not S, preventing
  incorrect double-wrapping of array types
- Revert unrelated space.ts changes (jsonFromValue/valueFromJson)
- Restore split test files (cell-*.test.ts, schema-*.test.ts)
- Add filterWithPattern and flatMapWithPattern test coverage
- Use import type for type-only imports in builtins
- Document type gap for .filter()/.flatMap() autocomplete in IDerivable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(runner): stop child pattern runs before clearing elementRuns

When the input list becomes undefined, stop each per-element pattern run
via runtime.runner.stop() before clearing the map. Previously, clearing
without stopping leaked active child executions in the reactive graph.

Addresses cubic-dev-ai review on #2977. The same pre-existing issue in
map.ts is left for a separate PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(runner): flatMap scalar semantics and cellMethods type safety

- flatMap now includes scalar (non-array) results directly, matching
  JS Array.prototype.flatMap semantics. undefined is still skipped
  due to two-pass convergence (new elements have undefined on first pass).
- Narrow cellMethods set type from `| string` to specific literal types
  so typos are caught at compile time.
- Add test for scalar flatMap results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(runner): add removal reconciliation and undefined cleanup tests

- Filter/flatMap reconciliation tests now verify element removal
  reuses existing runs with zero new evaluations.
- New test verifies that setting the list to undefined stops child
  pattern runs and produces empty output, and that restoring the
  list creates fresh runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(runner): add flatMap undefined cleanup test

Add missing test that verifies flatMap cleans up pattern runs when the
list becomes undefined and creates fresh runs when restored.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Mike Salisbury <mike@common.tools>

commit.message:
Fix resumeMigrate to detect and repair stale worktree paths

Two related recovery failures when a partially-migrated hub's parent
directory was renamed, or when worktrees ended up at wrong sub-paths:

1. The pending filter used `!startsWith(dest + '/')` which would mark
   worktrees at wrong sub-paths (e.g. dest/main-bare/feature instead of
   dest/feature) as "in place" and skip them entirely.

2. In the loop, when a worktree's registered path didn't exist, the tool
   skipped it with "path no longer exists" even if the worktree had
   already been moved to its expected destination (e.g. via a parent-dir
   rename that carried the worktrees along).

Fix: change the pending filter to `wt.path !== join(dest, branch)` (exact
match) so any worktree not at its exact expected location is processed.
In the loop, when the registered path is missing, fall back to the
expected destination path — if it exists there, call processLinkedWorktree
to repair the .git files in place (moveDir is a no-op when src === dest).

This avoids the need to manually move files and run sed to fix .git files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(deploy): prevent auto-advancing dead sessions as success (#77)

When the pod restarts, all tmux sessions die. Previously, the
orchestrator would find dead sessions, clear the session reference,
then auto-advance to the next stage assuming success. This caused
stages to be skipped entirely.

Now the orchestrator reads session output via Peek to determine
the actual outcome (success/fail/retry). When the tmux server is
unreachable (pod restart), it retries the stage instead of
assuming success.

Also switches registry secret from registry-personalcluster (read-only)
to personalcluster (read-write OAuth token) for Docker push support.

Co-authored-by: lucas <lucas@MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: increase silence timeout to 2500ms, bolder+darker line text

1000ms silence timeout triggered mid-line at natural sentence pauses.
2500ms gives breathing room for multi-sentence lines. SR instant advance
(75%) still handles the fast path. Font bumped to semibold black.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CDB9394 location backfill: use LATERAL join from tournament's own category

Previous migration failed due to category org_id mismatch and mode
space differences. New approach starts from the tournament's own
category_id, uses REPLACE for mode comparison, and LATERAL subquery
to find the best matching tournoi_ext entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace sv.LineZone with trip-wire PolygonZone for count lines

sv.LineZone fires only when the same tracker ID transitions across the
line in consecutive frames — impossible when YOLO detects each vehicle
in just 1-2 frames at intersection frame rates.

Both 2-point lines and 4-point polygons from the admin panel are now
converted to a thin rectangular PolygonZone band (±3% of frame height
perpendicular to the drawn line). The PolygonZone entry-counter fires
the first time any tracker ID's center enters the band, which works
reliably even with tf=1 sporadic detections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix comfort index: use setpoint range instead of single setpoint

In auto mode (both heat and cool setpoints), any temperature within the
heat-cool range now scores 100% on the temp component. Previously used
only setpoint_cool as target, penalizing temps in the middle of the
comfort zone (75°F in a 65-78 range scored 57% instead of 96%).

Modes handled:
- Auto: comfort range is [heat, cool], penalize outside range
- Heat only: penalize below setpoint, comfortable at or above
- Cool only: penalize above setpoint, comfortable at or below
- Off (no setpoints): returns None

commit.message:
feat(ui): expose Memory Node config fields in NodeConfigModal

Memory Node backend supports 10 config fields (operation, backend,
namespace, top_k, key, collection, persist_path, include_metadata)
but the UI only showed 'label'. Users had no way to configure ChromaDB
or switch between store/retrieve from the canvas.

Changes:
- Add handleIntChange + handleCheckboxChange helpers to NodeConfigModal
- Replace sparse Memory Node case (label only) with full config form:
  operation select (store/retrieve/delete/clear), backend select
  (sqlite_fts/chroma), namespace, top_k, key, collection + persist_path
  (chroma-only, conditionally shown), include_metadata checkbox
- Add NodeConfigModal.test.tsx with 8 tests covering Memory Node config:
  default values, chroma field visibility, field toggling, save payload,
  closed-state rendering (Gate 2 compliant — non-empty assertions on
  options arrays and saved data fields)

Tests: 109 frontend passed (+8), 1360 backend passed

commit.message:
fix(wordpress): transliterate filename to ASCII for Content-Disposition

Non-ASCII characters in article titles (e.g. Romanian ș, ă) caused
UnicodeEncodeError in the Content-Disposition header. Now uses NFKD
normalization + ASCII encoding to transliterate (e.g. "ș" → "s").

ci: add sequential Jules pipeline with auto-merge and throttling

  immediately after a Jules PR merges (no waiting for cron)

  daily-jules-knowledge.yml: skip issue creation if there's already
  an open Jules PR, preventing parallel work on accumulator files

Pipeline flow: issue created → Jules works → PR opened → quality
gates pass → auto-merge → trigger next issue → repeat

feat(apache): re-enable and redesign cockpit audio warning system

Replace queue-based audio with cooldown-gated direct playback.
Each hitpoint has its own cooldown so independent damage events
always play. Warnings only play for pilot and gunner, not wing
passengers. Missile warnings play clock direction + high/low as
an atomic pair. Damage PFH runs on pilot only with proper cleanup.

Also: add locality check to reorderWeapons, add docs/plans to
gitignore, and include prior uncommitted work across c130, f35,
reaper, common, and weapons addons.

commit.message:
Comprehensive questionnaire audit: 215 questions across 20 control families

- Added 22 new questions covering critical gaps:
  - PCI: vendor default passwords, service documentation, system hardening
  - SOC 2 Availability: SLAs, failover, redundant telco, capacity mgmt
  - SOC 2 Processing Integrity: data integrity checks, transaction verification
  - HIPAA: workforce security, PHI minimum necessary, disclosure accounting,
    patient amendment rights, secure PHI transmission, HIPAA training
  - PCI: cardholder data training
  - General: security function verification, error message hardening
- Fixed framework tags: nistcsf (0->219), iso (44->228), cmmc->201
- All 7 frameworks now have strong coverage (141-254 tagged questions)
- Removed temporary tag-fix utility scripts

commit.message:
Remove all Supabase realtime subscriptions for system stability

Removed 50+ realtime subscriptions across 31 files that were causing
race conditions, screen flickering, products auto-adding/removing in POS,
tabs closing on their own, and excessive refreshing when editing orders.
Only WhatsApp subscriptions are kept (inherently real-time messaging).

Changes:
- Phase 1: Hooks (14 files) - removed subscriptions, kept initial fetches
- Phase 2: Contexts (3 files) - removed CartContext syncWithDatabase after
  each operation, removed ThemeContext subscription, removed useRealCart
  setupRealtimeSubscription
- Phase 3: Components (8 files) - removed subscriptions from modals and
  tree views, kept data fetching on open/mount
- Phase 4: Pages (5 files) - removed subscriptions from POS, safes,
  suppliers, payment-methods, customer-orders pages
- Phase 5: Utility (1 file) - removed subscribeToCartChanges from
  cart-service.ts

commit.message:
fix: comprehensive dimmer teach-in - try ALL protocols and channels

Eltako FT55 pushbuttons use F6 Rocker AI (0x10), not BI (0x50).
Dimmer teach-in now tries 5 approaches (8 telegrams):
1. F6 AI press+release (what real pushbuttons send for dimmers)
2. A5 Command-2 with LRN=0 (central command teach-in)
3. A5 EEP A5-38-08 Eltako (standard 4BS teach-in)
4. F6 BI press+release (alternative rocker channel)
5. A5 Command-2 directed to actuator (non-broadcast fallback)

Added detailed logging for each attempt to help debugging.
